Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class CommunityThemeDefinition

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.CommunityThemeDefinition
public class CommunityThemeDefinition extends Metadata

Java class for CommunityThemeDefinition complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="CommunityThemeDefinition">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}Metadata">
       <sequence>
         <element name="bundlesInfo" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}CommunityThemeBundleInfo" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="customThemeLayoutType" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}CommunityCustomThemeLayoutType" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="defaultBrandingSet" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="description" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="enableExtendedCleanUpOnDelete" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="masterLabel" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="publisher" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="themeRouteOverride" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}CommunityThemeRouteOverride" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="themeSetting" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}CommunityThemeSetting" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getBundlesInfo

      public List<CommunityThemeBundleInfo> getBundlesInfo()
      Gets the value of the bundlesInfo property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the bundlesInfo property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getBundlesInfo().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list CommunityThemeBundleInfo

      Returns:
      The value of the bundlesInfo property.

    • getCustomThemeLayoutType

      public List<CommunityCustomThemeLayoutType> getCustomThemeLayoutType()
      Gets the value of the customThemeLayoutType property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the customThemeLayoutType property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getCustomThemeLayoutType().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list CommunityCustomThemeLayoutType

      Returns:
      The value of the customThemeLayoutType property.

      public List<CommunityThemeRouteOverride> getThemeRouteOverride()
      Gets the value of the themeRouteOverride property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the themeRouteOverride property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getThemeRouteOverride().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list CommunityThemeRouteOverride

      Returns:
      The value of the themeRouteOverride property.

      public List<CommunityThemeSetting> getThemeSetting()
      Gets the value of the themeSetting property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the themeSetting property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getThemeSetting().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list CommunityThemeSetting

      Returns:
      The value of the themeSetting property.
